steps.title: Multiply both sides to clear the fraction
steps.content:
Starting from $u - 4 = \frac{12}{t-3}$, multiply both sides by $(t-3)$:
$$(u-4)(t-3) = 12$$

steps.title: Solve for t
steps.content:
Divide both sides by $(u-4)$:
$$t - 3 = \frac{12}{u-4}$$
Add 3 to both sides:
$$t = 3 + \frac{12}{u-4} = \frac{3(u-4) + 12}{u-4} = \frac{3u - 12 + 12}{u-4} = \frac{3u}{u-4}$$
This matches choice D.
